name: Circuit des Barthes Hautes
description: Hike the moderate 7.5-mile Circuit des Barthes Hautes, gaining 293 feet in 3 hours 10 minutes, with Pyrenees views.
region_intro: Hiking around Saint-Barthélemy offers diverse topography, including coastal paths, rocky landscapes, and hills. The island features wild, rugged terrain with volcanic soil contrasting with lush vegetation. Trails often lead to secluded beaches, coves, and natural pools, providing varied scenery for outdoor exploration. Morne de Vitet, the island's highest point, offers expansive views. Best hiking trails around Saint-Barthélemy The most popular hiking route is Circuit des Barthes Hautes, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gravel Path Along the River – Fitness Trail loop from Urt, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This route follows a gravel path alongside a river. Local hikers also love the Fitness Trail – Gravel Path Along the River loop from Urcuit, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through a fitness trail area, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Hiking around Saint-Barthélemy is defined by coastal paths, rocky landscapes, and hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding ascents. The routes in Saint-Barthélemy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Saint-Barthélemy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: Saint-Barthélemy offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes range from easy strolls to more demanding ascents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: You can expect a varied landscape, including coastal paths with ocean views, rugged and rocky terrain often characterized by dark volcanic soil, and hills. Trails wind through lush vegetation, dense shrubland, and even cactus patches, leading to secluded beaches, coves, and natural pools.

Q: Are there trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Saint-Barthélemy has 15 easy hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41500579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fitness Trail – Gravel Path Along the River loop from Urcuit&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.5-mile trail that takes less than two hours, making it a good option for a leisurely walk. The island's varied terrain ensures options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there more challenging hikes available in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: For those seeking a challenge, there are 17 moderate routes. The ascent to Morne de Vitet, the island's highest point, offers cross-country paths through dense vegetation and rewards hikers with expansive 360-degree panoramic views. While not listed as difficult in our data, these routes can be demanding.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: The hiking routes in Saint-Barthélemy are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning coastal vistas, the wild and rugged natural beauty, and the opportunity to discover secluded beaches and natural pools.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993438975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circuit des Barthes Hautes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.5 miles long and takes approximately 3 hours and 10 minutes to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39253493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Path Along the River – Fitness Trail loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I see while hiking in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: The trails wind through diverse vegetation, offering chances to spot various bird species such as pelicans, egrets, herons, and gannets. You might also encounter wild goats, especially along coastal walks like the Toiny Beach Walk. The Saint-Barth Nature Reserve is a sanctuary for local wildlife and plant life.

Q: Are there hikes with great viewpoints in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: Absolutely. Many trails offer incredible views of the azure ocean and dramatic coastlines. The ascent to Morne de Vitet provides expansive 360-degree panoramic views of the island and neighboring islands. The short walk to Fort Karl from Gustavia Harbor also offers panoramic views of the harbor.

Q: Are there specific trails that lead to beaches or natural pools?
A: Yes, several hikes lead to secluded natural attractions. The popular Colombier Beach Hike, for instance, leads to a pristine white sand beach accessible only by foot or boat. The Grand Fond area is renowned for its wild, rocky landscape and hidden natural pools, which can be reached via the Grand Fond Trail (The Washing Machine Hike).

Q: What interesting places can I explore near the hiking trails?
A: Near the hiking trails, you can explore various points of interest. These include the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6631089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urt&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes d'Orx reserve&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1969064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marais d'Orx Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. There are also dedicated paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2666167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycle path to avoid the main road&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycle Path Along the Adour&lt;/a&gt; for alternative exploration.

Q: How long do hikes in Saint-Barthélemy typically take?
A: Hike durations vary significantly. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41499966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galupe Restaurant – Ardanavy pedestrian bridge loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 25 minutes. Longer, moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993438975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circuit des Barthes Hautes&lt;/a&gt; can take over 3 hours. It's advisable to check the estimated duration for each specific route.

Q: What should I wear or bring for hiking in Saint-Barthélemy?
A: Given the rocky and uneven paths, sturdy footwear is highly recommended. Light, breathable clothing is suitable for the tropical climate. It's also wise to bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and perhaps a swimsuit if you plan to visit any secluded beaches or natural pools.
tips: Enjoy breathtaking views of the start of the Pyrenees in the distance.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Circuit des Barthes Hautes hike?
faqs.answer: The Circuit des Barthes Hautes is rated as a moderate hike. It covers a distance of approximately 12.1 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 89 meters, making it accessible for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and low elevation gain, the trail likely features well-maintained paths through varied landscapes such as meadows, forests, and possibly some gentle slopes. You won't encounter rugged alpine conditions or significant technical challenges.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, one of the highlights of the route is a Beautiful view of the Pyrenees. This offers a scenic reward during your hike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Circuit des Barthes Hautes?
faqs.answer: On average, the Circuit des Barthes Hautes takes approximately 3 hours and 10 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Circuit des Barthes Hautes trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, most trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Circuit des Barthes Hautes?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike like this, spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) often offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, but be prepared for warmer weather.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Typically, local hiking trails like the Circuit des Barthes Hautes do not require permits or charge entrance fees. However, it's always good practice to check local regulations before your visit, especially if you plan to access any specific natural reserves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Circuit des Barthes Hautes trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Circuit des Barthes Hautes is not detailed, but trailheads in rural areas often have small designated parking areas or roadside parking nearby. Look for signs indicating trail access points.

faqs.question: Does the Circuit des Barthes Hautes intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You might find yourself on sections of the circuit_jaune_coteaux_barthes_smsx, circuit_rouge_coteaux_barthes_smsx, and circuit_bleu_coteaux_barthes_smsx, among others, for portions of the hike.

faqs.question: Is the Circuit des Barthes Hautes suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, relatively short distance, and low elevation gain, the Circuit des Barthes Hautes can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking. Always consider your children's fitness levels and bring plenty of water and snacks.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail for refreshments?
faqs.answer: While specific establishments aren't listed, the trail is likely situated near local villages. You can often find small cafes or restaurants in these areas to grab a bite or a drink before or after your hike.
